1.description: 1st Example of Medias’ Earnings News Coverage

2.description: 2nd Example of Medias’ Earnings News Coverage

3.description: Common Trends in the Levels of Total Deposits Notes. Figure 3 depicts the quarterly averages in the share of total deposits to total assets in the solid black line for the subsample of banks with net DTAs in the first quarter of 2016, that is, before the TCJA was expected. The dotted and gray line represents the subsample of banks without net DTAs in the first quarter of 2016. The gray area depicts the period between the end of Q4 2017 and the end of Q1 2018. In this period, the depositors learned about banks’ earnings that were affected by the DTA revaluations due to the TCJA and were expected to react to it.

4.description: Common Trends in the Change in Net Deferred Tax Assets Notes. Figure 4 depicts the quarterly averages in the changes of net DTAs from the previous quarter in the solid black line for the subsample of banks with net DTAs in the first quarter of 2016, that is, before the TCJA was expected. It further depicts the quarterly averages in the changes of net DTAs from the previous quarter with the dotted and gray line for the subsample of banks without net DTAs in the first quarter of 2016. The gray line depicts the period Q4 2017. In this period, banks’ earnings were affected by the DTA revaluations from the TCJA.

5.description: Media Tone on Bank Earnings Announcements Around the TCJA Notes. (a) Average tone of the earnings-related media coverage. It is the average media event sentiment score (ESS) for events that refer to bank earnings. (b) Probability of receiving media coverage with a negative tone on an earnings-related event. It is the probability of having at least one earnings-related media article in a quarter with an event sentiment score (ESS) that is below 50. The x axis refers to the period of the earnings information (e.g., Q4 2017) that is picked up in the media once the earnings gets released (e.g., Q1 2018).
